concerns.text: FR-009 requires a 'rubric' to verify 'high-quality human documentation' but does not define the rubric's criteria, scoring method, or pass/fail threshold. Without a defined rubric, the condition 'Human Docs' cannot be consistently selected or verified, making the requirement untestable.

FR-008 mandates 'model selection logic' and 'fallback to local model' but does not specify the trigger conditions for the fallback (e.g., specific HTTP status codes, latency thresholds) or the identity of the local model. This ambiguity prevents deterministic verification of the fallback behavior.

FR-004 requires logging 'clarification questions' but does not define what constitutes a 'clarification question' versus a general query or comment. Without a definition or classification rule, the count cannot be consistently measured or verified.

FR-007 sets performance constraints (≤6 hours, ≤7GB RAM) but does not specify the measurement environment (e.g., specific CPU model, cloud instance type) or the method of measurement (e.g., wall-clock time vs. CPU time). Performance is not reproducible without a defined reference environment.

SC-005 requires measuring execution time and memory against thresholds but does not specify the tool or method for measurement (e.g., `time`, `psutil`, specific profiler). The success criterion is unmeasurable without a defined measurement procedure.

User Story 1 states the system 'logs metrics' and a 'human moderator handles real-time guidance' but does not specify how the moderator's interventions are logged or if they are counted as part of the 'clarification questions' metric. The boundary between system-logged and human-logged data is ambiguous.

User Story 2 requires documentation to be generated 'within 15 minutes' but does not define the start and end points of this timer (e.g., from API call initiation to file write completion). The metric is not precisely measurable.

The edge case regarding 'stop-loss' mechanism states a moderator can 'record the task as failed with a maximum time cap' but does not define the value of the 'maximum time cap' or the specific conditions that trigger the stop-loss. This prevents verification of the edge case handling.
